summary_sections.title: Lesson Overview
summary_sections.content_html:
Overview

This lesson covers the fundamental measures of center used to analyze and summarize data sets. You'll learn how to calculate and apply the mean, median, and mode, and understand when each is most appropriate.

summary_sections.title: Definitions and Concepts
summary_sections.content_html:
Key Definitions

A piecewise function is defined as f(x) = 2x if x < 3, and f(x) = x + 5 if x >= 3. What is f(3)?

Since x = 3 satisfies x >= 3, use the second piece: f(3) = 3 + 5 = 8.

Using the same function: f(x) = 2x if x < 3, f(x) = x + 5 if x >= 3. What is f(1)?

Since x = 1 satisfies x < 3, use the first piece: f(1) = 2(1) = 2.

A step function assigns: f(x) = 1 if 0 <= x < 1, f(x) = 2 if 1 <= x < 2, f(x) = 3 if 2 <= x < 3. What is f(1.5)?

Since 1 <= 1.5 < 2, we use the second piece: f(1.5) = 2.

The greatest integer function (floor function) is written as f(x) = floor(x). What is floor(3.7)?

The greatest integer function returns the greatest integer less than or equal to x. floor(3.7) = 3.

A piecewise function is: f(x) = x^2 if x <= 0, f(x) = 3x - 1 if x > 0. What is f(-2)?

Since -2 <= 0, use the first piece: f(-2) = (-2)^2 = 4.

For the same function: f(x) = x^2 if x <= 0, f(x) = 3x - 1 if x > 0. What is f(4)?

Since 4 > 0, use the second piece: f(4) = 3(4) - 1 = 12 - 1 = 11.

What is floor(-2.3)?

The floor function returns the greatest integer less than or equal to x. floor(-2.3) = -3, because -3 is the greatest integer that is <= -2.3.

What is a piecewise function?

A piecewise function uses different formulas or expressions for different parts (intervals) of its domain. Each 'piece' of the function applies to a specific range of input values.

What is a step function?

A step function is a special type of piecewise function where each piece is a constant (horizontal segment). The graph looks like a staircase, jumping from one constant value to another.

What is the greatest integer function (floor function)?

The greatest integer function (floor function), written floor(x) or [x], returns the largest integer that is less than or equal to x. For example, floor(2.9) = 2 and floor(-1.5) = -2.

What is a discontinuity in a piecewise function?

A discontinuity occurs at a point where the graph has a break, jump, or gap. At this point, the left-hand and right-hand values of the function do not match or the function is not defined.

What is a boundary point in a piecewise function?

A boundary point is the x-value where one piece of the function ends and another begins. It is where the rule or formula changes. Evaluating at boundary points requires checking which piece applies.

What is the ceiling function?

The ceiling function returns the smallest integer that is greater than or equal to x. For example, ceiling(2.1) = 3 and ceiling(-1.5) = -1.

What is the absolute value function as a piecewise function?

The absolute value function |x| can be written as a two-piece piecewise function: f(x) = x when x >= 0 (right side of V) and f(x) = -x when x < 0 (left side of V), creating a V-shaped graph.

A parking garage charges $5 for the first hour or any part of it, plus $3 for each additional hour or part thereof. Using the ceiling function, the cost is C(t) = 5 + 3 * max(ceiling(t) - 1, 0) for t > 0. What is the cost for parking 3.5 hours?

Ceiling(3.5) = 4. Cost = 5 + 3*(4-1) = 5 + 9 = $14. You pay for 4 full hours since any partial hour is rounded up.

A cell phone plan charges $30 for up to 2 GB of data, $45 for more than 2 GB up to 5 GB, and $60 for more than 5 GB. This can be modeled as a step function. A user uses 3.5 GB. What is the cost?

3.5 GB is more than 2 but not more than 5, so the cost falls in the second tier: $45.

A shipping company charges based on weight: C(w) = 5 if 0 < w <= 1, C(w) = 8 if 1 < w <= 3, C(w) = 12 if 3 < w <= 5, C(w) = 20 if w > 5 (all in pounds, cost in dollars). A package weighs exactly 3 pounds. What is the shipping cost?

A package weighing exactly 3 pounds satisfies 1 < w <= 3 (since 3 <= 3 is true). The cost is $8.

A tax system uses piecewise rates: 10% on the first $10,000 of income, 20% on income from $10,001 to $40,000, and 30% on income above $40,000. What is the total tax on an income of $50,000?

First $10,000: 10% * 10000 = $1,000. Next $30,000 (10001-40000): 20% * 30000 = $6,000. Last $10,000 (40001-50000): 30% * 10000 = $3,000. Total: 1000 + 6000 + 3000 = $10,000.

A water utility charges: $0.005 per gallon for the first 5000 gallons, $0.008 per gallon for the next 10000 gallons, and $0.012 per gallon beyond 15000 gallons. A household uses 18000 gallons. What is the total bill?

First 5000: 0.005 * 5000 = $25. Next 10000 (5001-15000): 0.008 * 10000 = $80. Last 3000 (15001-18000): 0.012 * 3000 = $36. Total: 25 + 80 + 36 = $141.

A postage function charges based on letter weight in ounces: P(w) = $0.55 if 0 < w <= 1, P(w) = $0.55 + $0.15 * ceiling(w - 1) if w > 1. What is the postage for a letter weighing 3.2 ounces?

Since 3.2 > 1: ceiling(3.2 - 1) = ceiling(2.2) = 3. Postage = $0.55 + $0.15 * 3 = $0.55 + $0.45 = $1.00.

summary_sections.title: Real-World Applications
summary_sections.content_html:
Real-World Applications

Example: A parking garage charges $5 for the first hour or any part of it, plus $3 for each additional hour or part thereof. Using the ceiling function, the cost is C(t) = 5 + 3 * max(ceiling(t) - 1, 0) for t > 0. What is the cost for parking 3.5 hours?

Ceiling(3.5) = 4. Cost = 5 + 3*(4-1) = 5 + 9 = $14. You pay for 4 full hours since any partial hour is rounded up.

Example: A shipping company charges based on weight: C(w) = 5 if 0 < w <= 1, C(w) = 8 if 1 < w <= 3, C(w) = 12 if 3 < w <= 5, C(w) = 20 if w > 5 (all in pounds, cost in dollars). A package weighs exactly 3 pounds. What is the shipping cost?

A package weighing exactly 3 pounds satisfies 1 < w <= 3 (since 3 <= 3 is true). The cost is $8.

Example: A piecewise function models a student's grade: f(x) = 4.0 if 90 <= x <= 100, f(x) = 3.0 if 80 <= x < 90, f(x) = 2.0 if 70 <= x < 80, f(x) = 1.0 if 60 <= x < 70, f(x) = 0 if x < 60. A student scores exactly 80. What GPA points do they earn?

A score of 80 satisfies 80 <= x < 90, so the student earns 3.0 GPA points.

Example: A postage function charges based on letter weight in ounces: P(w) = $0.55 if 0 < w <= 1, P(w) = $0.55 + $0.15 * ceiling(w - 1) if w > 1. What is the postage for a letter weighing 3.2 ounces?

Since 3.2 > 1: ceiling(3.2 - 1) = ceiling(2.2) = 3. Postage = $0.55 + $0.15 * 3 = $0.55 + $0.45 = $1.00.
